title: lichen_sha256 inline in header causes code bloat
description:
lichen_util.h:41-54: lichen_sha256() is static inline in a header. It's 14 lines including struct allocation, three function calls, and secure_zero.

What goes wrong:
1. Every translation unit that includes lichen_util.h and calls lichen_sha256 gets its own copy
2. Currently called from: lora_l2.c (generate_eui64), ipv6_addr.c (lichen_pubkey_to_iid)
3. Each call site gets ~100+ bytes of code (SHA init/update/final + cleanup)
4. Embedded systems are flash-constrained

The secure_zero() inline is appropriate (few bytes, called from many places). But lichen_sha256() should be in a .c file.

Fix: Move lichen_sha256() to lichen_util.c (may need to create it) and declare extern in header.
close_reason: Fixed: Moved lichen_sha256() from static inline in header to extern declaration with implementation in new lichen_util.c file
